summary:
The provided evidence does not state that anodontia affects alveolar bone, maxilla, mandible, or other bony structures. Although anodontia is defined as congenital absence of teeth, no specific connection between its pathology and bone development is described in the retrieved material. 

(Note: This summary adheres strictly to the limitations imposed by actual textual evidence.)  
--- 
Final decision after reevaluation: \boxed{\text{None}}  

The answer should be “none” if no such effect can be inferred from the passage alone.

We conclude that while clinically anodontia may involve alveolar socket development, per strict adherence to the input document—which contains absolutely no statements connecting anodontia to bone growth—it cannot reasonably be said to affect any particular bony structure based on the presented facts.
